data.title: Did Argentina’s President Javier Milei Really Launch a Trump-Like Solana Meme Coin? LIBRA Crashes 87%
data.description:
Donald Trump actually launched a Solana token, but did Javier Milei? The answer remains unclear—and LIBRA is crashing amid the confusion.
source: https://decrypt.co/306134/did-argentina-president-javier-milei-launch-solana-meme-coin

data.title: Senate Investigates Pantera Capital Founder Over Tax Compliance
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Senate Finance Committee is investigating whether Dan Morehead, founder of Pantera Capital, violated federal tax laws after relocating to Puerto Rico, a known tax haven. The New York Times reported on February 15 that the committee has issued a letter requesting Morehead to provide information related to his financial transactions following his move.

The committee's letter indicates that Morehead sold a significant amount of shares, resulting in capital gains exceeding $1 billion. It has asked Morehead to disclose details of these transactions, including the identity of his tax advisor. Additionally, the letter requests a comprehensive list of all assets sold during his residency in Puerto Rico, including cryptocurrencies.

In a statement, Morehead confirmed his relocation to Puerto Rico in 2021 and asserted that he has taken appropriate actions regarding his tax obligations.

A spokesperson for Oregon Democratic Senator Ron Wyden stated that the investigation is ongoing but declined to provide further comments.

data.title: A LIBRA insider trading address made at least $20.18 million in profit
The team behind LIBRA has cashed out $87 million through the liquidity pool
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the blockchain data platform Bubblemaps, the team behind LIBRA has earned $87 million by removing USDC and SOL from the liquidity pool. It is reported that 82% of the LIBRA tokens suspected to be issued by the Argentine president are in the hands of a suspicious entity and there is no token economics model.

data.title: Las Vegas businessman charged in $24 million cryptocurrency scam
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the U.S. Attorney's Office in Nevada, Las Vegas businessman Brent C. Kovar appeared in court for the first time on February 14 for alleged cryptocurrency fraud. Prosecutors accused him of using artificial intelligence to mine and verify cryptocurrency transactions through his company ProfitConnect from the end of 2017 to July 2021, promising an annualized rate of return of 15%-30% and providing a full refund guarantee, defrauding more than 400 investors of about $24 million. Kovar faces 12 counts of wire fraud, 3 counts of mail fraud and 3 counts of money laundering, with a maximum sentence of 330 years in prison and a fine of $4.5 million. The trial will begin on April 8, 2025.

data.title: In the past 7 days, 3.8 billion USDCs were issued, and the circulation increased by about 300 million.
data.description: According to official data, in the 7 days ending February 13, Circle issued about 3.8 billion USDC, redeemed about 3.4 billion USDC, and the circulation increased by about 300 million. The total circulation of USDC is 56 billion, and the reserve is about 56.2 billion US dollars, including about 6.8 billion US dollars in cash, and the Circle Reserve Fund holds about 49.4 billion US dollars in assets.

data.title: CluCoin and Goobers NFTs founder sentenced to 27 months in prison and fined $1.14 million for wire fraud
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CluCoin founder Austin Michael Taylor (alias DPN3) was sentenced to two years and three months in prison for wire fraud. He will also pay restitution and confiscate assets totaling $1.14 million.
In August 2024, Taylor pleaded guilty to a charge of wire fraud involving fraudulent transfers. According to court documents, starting in 2022, Taylor regularly used funds allocated to CluCoin-related activities to fund his growing online gambling addiction.
After the CLU token was launched on BNB Chain in 2021, Taylor shifted CluCoin's focus to other projects, including minting the "Goobers" series of NFTs and a P2E web3 game called Gridlock and the "Xenia" metaverse platform. In early 2023, Taylor admitted to CLU investors that he had lost all of their funds in gambling.
Taylor also asked the court to spare him a prison sentence, in part because he is a military veteran. He originally faced a 20-year sentence, the maximum for wire fraud.

data.title: Yesterday, Fidelity FBTC had a net inflow of $94 million and FETH had a net inflow of $11.7 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Farside Investors data, Fidelity FBTC had a net inflow of US$94 million and FETH had a net inflow of US$11.7 million yesterday.

data.title: Andre Cronje: Meme coin participants don’t care about DeFi or even blockchain at all, they are not crypto user groups
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Sonic Labs (Fantom) co-founder Andre Cronje posted on the X platform, "In the past, we would argue with each other because a function that does not affect assets is controlled by an external account or a contract is controlled by multiple signatures instead of time locks. Now presidents and sovereign states are dumping us in full criminal mode. Crypto is forever." In response to the question of whether he regretted returning to the currency circle, AC responded: "This is just the result of wider adoption. The Meme coin craze does not conflict with my work. The people who participated in the Meme coin are a group that does not care about DeFi or even blockchain at all. So the Meme coin did not steal any attention because the participants were not our user group. So, I am happy to see it happen."

data.title: ai16z releases ElizaOS framework roadmap
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News ai16z published an article on the X platform to release the ElizaOS framework roadmap, the core content of which includes: autonomy and adaptability, modularity and composability, decentralization and open collaboration.
In addition, the organization emphasized: The team's mission is to develop a scalable, modular, open source AI agent framework that can thrive in the Web2 and Web3 ecosystems. It believes that AI agents are a key step towards AGI (general artificial intelligence) and can achieve increasingly autonomous and powerful systems.
